desc: The One-Sample t-test analyse whether the mean of a population is statistically different from a known or hypothetical value. This is a parametric test, so assumptions must be validated before performing the test. The null hypothesis (H0) is that the population mean equals the sample mean (known or hypothetical value).

The Two-Samples Students t-test (also known as the independent-samples t-test) is a parametric test used to test whether the unknown population means of two groups are equal or not. This test is used when the two small samples (n < 30) are drawn from two different populations and compared. The standard null hypothesis (H0) of a Two-Sample Students t-Test is that the two groups are equal.

This test is used to test the mean of a sample when normal distribution is not assumed. The Wilcoxon test can be an alternative to the t-test, especially when the sample data does not follow a normal distribution. It is a non-parametric (or distribution-free) test used to test whether an estimate is different from its true value. The Wilcoxon test is used to test the null hypothesis that the mean value of a distribution is equal to some value.
This test is used to validate whether a sample of data follows a normal distribution. The null hypothesis (H0) is that the sample follows a normal distribution. This is a non-parametric test.

The Kolmogorov-Smirnov Test is a hypothesis test to determine whether two samples of data are from the same distribution. The test is non-parametric and completely agnostic as to what that distribution actually is. The null hypothesis ( H0) is that both samples come from a population with the same distribution.
